aliases: King Witiza (visigothic king mentioned in narratives)
biography_short: Rey visigodo cuyo reinado y faction are invoked in narratives explaining the internal divisions that facilitated Muslim entry; presented in sources as a focal point for later factional conflicts.

visual_profile.clothing: royal visigothic garb as depicted in chronicles; formal robe
visual_profile.style_notes: use iconographic cues of late-Visigothic royalty and courtly factionalism.

must_keep: Referenced as a cause of internal divisions and as a ruler whose party later aligns with invaders in certain accounts
must_avoid: Presenting legendary anecdotes as verified facts without labeling them as tradition

emotional_state.trigger_summary: His reign and factional policies are cited as contributing factors to the later breakdown of Visigothic cohesion.

arc_state.internal_need: secure succession and loyal noble support
arc_state.contradiction: internal noble fragmentation vs. central authority
arc_state.change_vector: reign -> factional legacy facilitating external intervention
